title: 생성에 실패했습니다
fallbackMessage: 이번 생성은 완료되지 않았습니다. API 대기열이 길거나, 동시에 사용하는 사용자가 너무 많거나, 별지도 풀이에 현재 사용 가능한 것보다 더 많은 연산 자원이 필요할 수 있습니다. 잠시 후 다시 시도해 주세요.

plateSolveFailed.title: Couldn't recognize the sky in this image
plateSolveFailed.message: The astrometric solver couldn't match any constellations. This usually means the image isn't a single raw exposure of the night sky. A few common causes:

panorama.title: Stitched panorama
panorama.detail: multi-frame panoramas warp star positions so pattern matching fails.

composite.title: Multi-exposure composite
composite.detail: blending sky and foreground from separate shots (e.g. star-tracker + static foreground) distorts the geometry.

fisheye.title: Fisheye or extreme wide-angle
fisheye.detail: very wide or uncorrected fisheye lenses break the linear projection the solver relies on.

heavyEditing.title: Heavy post-processing
heavyEditing.detail: stacked, denoised, or AI-enhanced photos often lose the precise star positions.

notNightSky.title: Not a night-sky photo
notNightSky.detail: screenshots, daytime shots, planetarium renders, or illustrations are not supported.

tooFewStars.title: Too few stars visible
tooFewStars.detail: heavy cloud cover, light pollution, or underexposure leaves too few points to match.

motionBlur.title: Motion blur or star trails
motionBlur.detail: long exposures without tracking turn stars into streaks, which the solver can't identify.

plateSolveFailed.suggestion: Try uploading a single unedited exposure of the night sky. If you have a wide panorama, crop out a smaller central region and upload that instead.
